name: Scenario
what_it_does: AI game asset generation platform with a REST API for creating consistent 2D and 3D game art at scale by fine-tuning models on existing art styles.
use_cases: Fine-tune a custom image generation model on existing game art to produce style-consistent characters, items, and environments
Generate large batches of game asset variants via API for agent-driven game development pipelines
Create UI elements, icons, and environmental textures that match an established visual style
Iterate rapidly on character designs and item concepts during pre-production without manual artist time
not_for: Photorealistic product photography or marketing imagery; Scenario is tuned for stylized game art
Teams needing real-time image generation at sub-second latency for interactive applications
best_when: Best when indie or mid-size game studios need to produce large volumes of style-consistent 2D assets without scaling an art team.
avoid_when: Avoid when your art pipeline requires hand-crafted assets with full artistic control rather than AI-generated variations.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in the Authorization header. Keys are per-account with no scope granularity. Generated from the Scenario dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 100 images per month on the free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $23/month for higher generation volumes and advanced features
pricing.notes: Free tier is suitable for evaluation. Production pipelines generating high asset volumes will require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent generation jobs are limited by plan tier; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns JSON error responses with descriptive messages. Generation failure reasons are surfaced in job status responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each generation API call creates a new job and is not idempotent; agents should check for existing completed jobs before re-triggering generation.
known_agent_gotchas: Image generation is asynchronous; agents must poll job status or use webhooks rather than expecting synchronous results
Fine-tuned model training takes minutes to hours; agents must handle long-running async workflows for model creation
No official SDK means agents must implement polling loops and webhook handling manually
Generated images are stored temporarily; agents must download and store assets before the hosted URL expires

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope granularity is a weak point; key exposure grants full account access. TLS enforced. No compliance certifications publicly documented.
